page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance family virtual machine featuring 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. It operates on the x86_64 architecture with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 500 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and FMA.

Benchmark evaluations place this server's processing capabilities in the average tier. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 2219.48, and the multi-core performance reaches 27275.02. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block speed of 1019.76 GB/sec, though large block uncached read speeds drop to 79.14 GB/sec, and write speeds are measured at 44.07 GB/sec. Passmark memory testing indicates a weaker memory performance tier with a score of 1623.4. Single-threaded compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3 decompression, perform poorly, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression recovers to an average tier at 41.52 GB/sec.

Given its 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is structured for compute-leaning workloads that do not demand massive memory footprints. It achieves average throughput in database operations, scoring 3550.56 on Passmark database tests and handling 11.99 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. Static web serving throughput is stable at 7.88 GB/sec. For machine learning, it handles small-model LLM inference at average speeds, but struggles with prompt processing on larger 7B models. This server offers a cost-efficient profile for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and moderate database workloads where shared CPU resources and local NVMe storage are acceptable trade-offs.
description: The Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 12 vCPUs and 12 physical cores on a shared AMD EPYC-Rome processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, it is equipped with 24.0 GB of RAM and 500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks generally fall into the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 27275.02 and a Passmark CPU score of 17153.85. However, memory performance is relatively weak, as indicated by a Passmark memory score of 1623.4 and poor single-threaded compression results. With a 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations that benefit from local NVMe storage without requiring dedicated CPU cores or high memory density.
og_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 12 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 24 GB of RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced multi-core performance for general compute workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-amd is a High Performance server with 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A multi-core virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for compute-leaning workloads.
bullet_points: 12 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
24.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Average multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and moderate databases
